responses.rawOutput: {"final_answer":["1. No, the sprinklers do not run this cycle because the tank is at 15% (below 20%).","2. Yes, the refill valve activates because the pump redirects to it when tank level is at or below 20%.","3. With the rain sensor removed, the timer activates the pump at 6 AM regardless of rain. The pump draws from the tank, finds it at 15% (below 20%), so it activates the refill valve and no water goes to the sprinklers. Same outcome as the current scenario."],"confidence":0.97,"working":["Q1: Rain sensor reports NO rain → timer activates pump at 6 AM. Pump draws from tank. Tank at 15% ≤ 20%, so pump activates refill valve instead of sprinklers. Sprinklers do NOT run.","Q2: Since tank ≤ 20%, refill valve activates to fill tank from mains. YES.","Q3: Without rain sensor, timer always activates pump. Pump checks tank: 15% ≤ 20%, so refill valve activates, sprinklers don't run. The rain status is irrelevant since sensor is removed. Result: refill valve activates, no sprinklers."]}

final_answer: 1. No, the sprinklers do not run this cycle because the tank is at 15% (below 20%).
2. Yes, the refill valve activates because the pump redirects to it when tank level is at or below 20%.

3. With the rain sensor removed, the timer activates the pump at 6 AM regardless of rain. The pump draws from the tank, finds it at 15% (below 20%), so it activates the refill valve and no water goes to the sprinklers. Same outcome as the current scenario.
working: Q1: Rain sensor reports NO rain → timer activates pump at 6 AM. Pump draws from tank. Tank at 15% ≤ 20%, so pump activates refill valve instead of sprinklers. Sprinklers do NOT run.
Q2: Since tank ≤ 20%, refill valve activates to fill tank from mains. YES.

Q3: Without rain sensor, timer always activates pump. Pump checks tank: 15% ≤ 20%, so refill valve activates, sprinklers don't run. The rain status is irrelevant since sensor is removed. Result: refill valve activates, no sprinklers.
